name: Canonico, Chris

Critically, the fec_donation_count of 46 on this record is NOT the usual false-match artifact: a query restricted to 'Christopher Canonico' in Texas returns exactly 46 results, and their employer history walks through MWH (Dallas, 2003) to Camp Dresser & McKee Inc. as 'Client Officer' (Bellaire 77401, 2005-2006) to Ardurra — which reproduces his LinkedIn career history (MWH, then CDM Smith, then Ardurra) precisely. name: Jones, Doug

PARTNER' — an exact match to this filing's zip (78731), and HPI Real Estate is precisely the firm Doug Jones left in 2014, alongside Volney Campbell and Marc Vanderslice, to launch the Colliers Austin affiliate office. This filing's employer ('Colliers') and occupation ('Brokerage') capture where that move landed him; his LinkedIn is registered as 'Doug Jones Jr.', matching the FEC suffix. He began brokering in 2006, grew the Colliers office from three people in an executive suite to fifteen by 2019, sold the affiliate to corporate Colliers in January 2020, and has closed 725+ transactions totalling 8.1 million square feet and over $1 billion in value. The fec_partisan_lean of 0.0 and fec_donation_count of 1 are genuine but rest on that single 2011 data point — the $250 went to Michael Williams for Congress, a Republican — so the 'lean' is one gift fifteen years ago, not a pattern. The other Austin 'Doug/Douglas Jones' records in FEC belong to different people (an Intel firmware architect at 78738 giving to Romney in 2012; two students at 78739 giving to Obama and the DNC in 2008) and are not him. Worth noting the cross-party shape: his only federal gift was Republican, while this $400 went to Democrat Kirk Watson — consistent with an Austin commercial broker giving to the mayor on business rather than ideological grounds. No AIPAC, pro-Israel, oil & gas, gun or defense affiliation surfaced.

evidence: The filing itself carries an unambiguous employer and occupation — 'Westminster Presbyterian School' and 'Office Administration' — which resolves to Westminster Presbyterian Day School, a licensed cooperative preschool in Tarrytown, west Austin, serving roughly 85-90 children ages 15 months through five and operated out of Westminster Presbyterian Church at 3208 Exposition Blvd. Administrative staff at a preschool classifies as Education rather than Nonprofit / Advocacy. The person is corroborated through her household: the donor's 78731 (Northwest Hills) zip matches exactly the zip on the federal record for Rob Golding of Austin 78731, employer 'Rodeo Austin', occupation 'Executive', who gave $250 to Tammy Young for Congress in February 2020 — and Rodeo Austin's own coverage of his hiring states that 'Golding and his wife Nan and two children have called Austin home for more than 35 years,' tying a Nan Golding to that exact 78731 household. Rob Golding spent a decade as chairman and CEO of Austin real-estate developer Live Oak Gottesman before taking over Rodeo Austin in 2016 and retiring from it effective September 2022, so the household's civic and business standing makes a $400 gift to Kirk Watson's mayoral campaign unremarkable. The one traceable federal datapoint in the household, Rob Golding's gift to a Democrat, is at least consistent with a Democratic-leaning household giving to Watson.

A MUD developer and former City of Austin utility staffer who has hired lobbyists giving to Kirk Watson's mayoral campaign is worth flagging as a land-use/regulatory relationship rather than ordinary constituent giving. name: Lindenmuth, Gerald

(510 Hearn St, Austin 78703) — an oil & gas producer/operator incorporated in Texas in 1984 with 28 wells across Texas, Oklahoma, New Mexico and Michigan; also president of the Lindenmuth Family Foundation and, per his own federal filings, a board member of IBC Bank
evidence: Gerald S. Lindenmuth is the owner/president of Lindenmuth and Associates, Inc., an Austin oil & gas producer at 510 Hearn Street in 78703 — the same 78703 zip as this donor record, and the same employer string and 'President' title the filing carries. Federal records tie the identification down precisely: FEC shows GERALD LINDENMUTH at AUSTIN, TX 787034516 giving $5,000 to the NRSC (2024 and 2022), $5,000 via WinRed (2022), $2,800 to the Senate Georgia Battleground Fund and $1,400 each to Perdue for Senate and Georgians for Kelly Loeffler (2020), self-reporting as 'Self-Employed / Engineer'; campaignmoney's record of his $2,800 gift to Gardner Tillis Victory in 2019 lists the same Austin 78703 address with occupation 'Oil & Gas Producer,' which is what fixes the industry. The oil & gas business is well documented independently: shalexp's operator profile for Lindenmuth & Associates shows 2,018,589 barrels of oil and 26,107,844 MCF of gas produced from January 1982 to June 2025 across 28 now-plugged or non-producing wells in Michigan, New Mexico, Oklahoma and Texas, and mineralholders separately lists Gerald Lindenmuth personally holding 8 mineral interests in Pecos and Upton counties operated by Chevron Midcontinent, Pioneer Natural Resources USA and Sahara Operating. Unlike most records in this dataset, the fec_partisan_lean of 0.0 across 23 donations is a genuine match rather than an artifact — every verified federal gift is Republican — which makes the $400 to Kirk Watson, a Democrat, a real cross-party gift worth noting. Confidence is high because name, zip (78703-4516), employer and title all corroborate exactly. One caveat carried into the affiliations below: the IBC Bank board seat rests on his own FEC-reported employer/occupation ('IBC BANK' / 'BOARD MEMBER,' alongside a $5,000 gift to IBC's corporate PAC) and could not be corroborated against IBC's published Austin board roster; likewise, Lindenmuth Realty ('the real estate arm of a Texas-based family office with two generations and 40 years of ownership behind it' — consistent with the firm's 1984 incorporation) does not name its principals, so that link is probable but unconfirmed.

name: Benyousef, Malek Ben-Musa
resolved_employer: Head coach and sporting director, Round Rock SC (Round Rock Soccer Club) — a community youth soccer club founded 2017 serving U4-U23 with recreational, academy, UPSL and USL League Two semi-pro teams; since then co-founder/co-owner of Austin Sol FC and office manager at FBC | Generosity Experts
evidence: This is Malek Ben-Musa Benyousef, who coaches professionally under the name Malek Ben-Musa: a coach of 35+ years from youth through collegiate to the pro level (assistant coach with the USL Championship's Austin Bold), holder of a specialized master's in Soccer Coaching from Ohio University (2016-17), and — exactly matching this record's 'Head Coach' occupation and 'Round Rock Soccer Club' employer at the time of the March 2022 gift — the former head coach and sporting director of Round Rock SC's USL League Two side. The zip corroborates precisely: his wife's firm's team page states that 'Malek resides in Lakeway with his wife, Fayruz Benyousef, and their daughter, Yara,' and Lakeway is 78738, the zip on this record. The federal data independently supports the household without contradicting anything: fec_donation_count of 0 is accurate — Malek has no federal record of his own — but a nationwide FEC search for 'Benyousef' returns exactly 5 results, all of them Fayruz Benyousef at Austin 78738-1802, giving to Doggett for Congress ($1,000 in both 2020 and 2022), ActBlue, Eddie for Texas and Sarver for Texas, a Democratic household pattern consistent with a $400 gift to Kirk Watson. He is now co-founder/co-owner of Austin Sol FC and serves as Vice President of Mentorship for 100 Black Men of Austin. Confidence is high on identity — name, occupation, employer and zip all line up. The industry label is a taxonomy judgment rather than a research gap: the list has no sports category, and Round Rock SC is a community youth club (U4-U19 recreational soccer with no tryouts, after-school programs run with local school districts) that also fields semi-pro sides, so Nonprofit / Advocacy fits it better than Entertainment — though note that RRSC's own site and its Givebutter donation page never state a 501(c)(3) status, and the separately registered Round Rock Soccer Association Inc (EIN 74-2035819, a 501(c)(3) amateur-sports org) cannot be confirmed as the same entity, since RRSC dates itself to 2017. One incidental note recorded for transparency but deliberately NOT entered as an affiliation: press coverage lists Shalom Austin, the Jewish federation organization, among the past clients of Fayruz Benyousef Consulting, the fundraising consultancy his wife founded and where he now works as office manager. That is a client relationship of his employer, not a personal membership, board seat or affiliation of his, and treating it as one would misrepresent him.

Classified Architecture per the standing convention in this dataset, which places interior and furnishing designers (Dawn Thompson Interiors, Kristen Nix Interiors, Debra Hurt Interior Design) under that label rather than Retail or Self-Employed. Held at medium rather than high for one reason: every published source calls him simply 'Tyler Hill,' and no source confirms 'Richard' as his legal first name, so the first name in this filing is unverified — though using a middle name professionally is unremarkable, and no competing Richard/Tyler Hill designer in DC surfaced. The fec_donation_count of 0 is accurate rather than a matching failure: an FEC search for employer 'Transparent' returns 1,734 records, none of them a Hill in DC, and a search for contributors named Hill in Washington, DC with occupation 'Designer' returns 122 records belonging entirely to Johnny Hill, a graphic designer at Marcom Group in 20003 making $3-$25 ActBlue-style gifts — a different person with a different zip, employer and giving profile. Her federal giving history places her at Austin, TX 78731 — an exact match to the donor zip — with employer 'Not Employed' and occupation 'Mother,' matching the filed 'Not-employed' occupation and employer; her 2020 cycle alone shows $5,600 across two maxed gifts, $2,800 to Texans for Senator John Cornyn Inc. and $2,800 to WinRed. Beyond Batten Disease Foundation, the Austin nonprofit fighting juvenile Batten disease, lists 'Stacy and Michael Toomey' on its development board and 'The Honorable and Mrs. Mike Toomey' in its Founders Circle — the honorific confirming the former state representative and tying Stacy to him. Unlike most records in this batch, the fec_partisan_lean of 0.0 across 20 donations appears to be a true match rather than an artifact: the verified Cornyn and WinRed gifts are uniformly Republican and are filed from the matching zip under the matching occupation. Industry is 'Not Employed' rather than an inferred former career, since no prior profession for her could be documented and the retiree rule does not apply to a homemaker. The politically notable feature is the cross-current: a household at the center of Texas Republican power gave $400 to Kirk Watson, a former Democratic state senator, in Austin's nonpartisan 2022 mayoral race — a pattern consistent with lobby-adjacent giving to a likely city executive rather than with her federal partisan record. Recorded below is her husband's oil-and-gas-adjacent lobbying, flagged explicitly as a spousal affiliation and not the donor's own.
